symptoms.symptom_name: Known abnormally rapid increase in head circumference (under investigation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pre-existing rapidly enlarging head is the substrate on which acute decompensation occurs; it reframes the new symptoms as raised ICP.
A high-pitched 'cerebral' cry is a recognised infant sign of neurological distress / raised ICP.
Colic cry is paroxysmal and settles; a continuous, uncharacteristic high-pitched cry with other neuro signs is not colic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Forceful non-bilious projectile vomiting with feed refusal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central (projectile, non-nausea-driven) vomiting is a classic feature of raised ICP.
Gastroenteritis vomiting is usually preceded by nausea/diarrhoea and is not accompanied by a tense fontanelle or sunsetting eyes.
A fontanelle that stays tense and bulging when the infant is calm and upright is a strong sign of raised ICP. (Carer-observable; true tension requires clinician palpation — see sealed physical sign.)

symptoms.symptom_name: Sunsetting eyes (persistent downward gaze, sclera visible above iris)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The sunsetting sign reflects dorsal midbrain compression (Parinaud) from raised ICP and is highly specific in an infant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive lethargy, pallor and reduced responsiveness between crying spells

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A declining conscious level is a late, ominous sign of raised ICP and impending herniation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prominent scalp veins and head large for body

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distended scalp veins and relative macrocephaly reflect chronically raised intracranial volume with venous congestion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cushing's triad — systolic hypertension, relative bradycardia, irregular respiration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cushing's triad indicates brainstem compression and imminent herniation — clinician-measured; the AI infers deterioration from pallor and falling consciousness, not from measured vitals.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widely separated cranial sutures / head circumference >99th percentile on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suture diastasis and a >99th-percentile head confirm chronically raised intracranial volume — a clinician measurement, sealed.

investigation_details.expected_result: Dilated ventricles confirming hydrocephalus; identifies the level/cause of obstruction
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cause and severity of the CSF obstruction cannot be confirmed and CSF diversion cannot be planned.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ventricular dilation on imaging is the confirmatory finding — hospital-only, never available via telehealth.
Afebrile status makes meningitis a little less likely, but does NOT exclude it — an afebrile infant can still have meningitis, so it does not lower the escalation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Projectile vomiting + tense bulging fontanelle + sunsetting eyes + declining consciousness together are a time-critical raised-ICP emergency in an infant.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency non-contrast CT / MRI brain (at hospital)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms ventricular dilation and guides emergency CSF diversion — arranged by the receiving neurosurgical team, not the telehealth clinician.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Attempting to measure vitals or examine the fontanelle over chat
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not obtainable via telehealth and must not delay the 000 call; escalation does not wait on remote measurement.
